questions.q: The Elephanta Caves are located on an island off the coast of which Indian city?
questions.explanation: The Elephanta Caves lie on Elephanta Island (Gharapuri) in Mumbai Harbour, off the coast of Mumbai.

questions.q: The principal caves at Elephanta are primarily dedicated to which Hindu deity?
questions.explanation: The main cave complex at Elephanta is a Shaiva site dedicated chiefly to the god Shiva.

questions.q: In which Indian state are the Elephanta Caves situated?
questions.explanation: The Elephanta Caves are in the state of Maharashtra, within Mumbai Harbour.

questions.q: How were the Elephanta Caves created?
questions.explanation: The caves are rock-cut, excavated directly out of the solid basalt rock of the hillside.

questions.q: The Elephanta Caves are listed as a site of what international status?
questions.explanation: The Elephanta Caves were inscribed as a UNESCO World Heritage Site in 1987.

questions.q: The most famous sculpture at Elephanta is a giant three-headed image known as the Trimurti. What does it represent?
options: The Hindu trinity of three gods
Three forms or aspects of Shiva
questions.explanation: The colossal Trimurti depicts three aspects of Shiva carved as a single three-faced bust.

questions.q: What is the local name of the island on which the Elephanta Caves are located?
questions.explanation: The island is known locally as Gharapuri, while 'Elephanta' is a name given by Portuguese visitors.

questions.q: How do most visitors typically travel from Mumbai to Elephanta Island?
questions.explanation: Visitors reach Elephanta Island by ferry, usually departing from the Gateway of India in Mumbai.

questions.q: The name 'Elephanta' is derived from a large statue of which animal that early European visitors saw on the island?
questions.explanation: The Portuguese named the island after a large stone elephant statue they found near the landing site.

questions.q: The Elephanta Caves are best described as which type of religious architecture?
questions.explanation: Elephanta consists of rock-cut cave temples hewn into the basalt rock of the island hill.

questions.q: Which European colonial power gave the island the name 'Elephanta' after seeing a stone elephant statue?
questions.explanation: Portuguese explorers named the island Elephanta after the stone elephant they encountered there in the 16th century.

questions.q: What type of rock were the Elephanta Caves carved into?
questions.explanation: The caves were excavated from the hard volcanic basalt that forms the island's hills.

questions.q: Approximately which centuries are most commonly given for the main phase of excavation of the great Elephanta cave?
questions.explanation: Scholars generally date the main Shiva cave to roughly the mid-1st millennium CE, around the 5th to 8th centuries.

questions.q: The Trimurti and other panels at Elephanta primarily celebrate the legends and forms of which deity?
questions.explanation: The sculptural program centers on Shiva, depicting his many forms and mythological episodes.

questions.q: Which of these famous Shaiva sculptural panels can be found in the main Elephanta cave?
questions.explanation: The cave includes the celebrated Ardhanarishvara panel showing Shiva united with Parvati as a single half-male, half-female figure.

questions.q: In which year were the Elephanta Caves inscribed on the UNESCO World Heritage List?
questions.explanation: UNESCO inscribed the Elephanta Caves as a World Heritage Site in 1987.

questions.q: The Trimurti sculpture at Elephanta is best described in scale as which of the following?
options: A small relief about a metre tall
A colossal carving several metres high
A miniature panel set in the floor
questions.explanation: The Trimurti is a monumental, colossal bust roughly six metres tall carved into the rock wall.

questions.q: Besides the Hindu Shaiva caves, what other kind of rock-cut caves also exist on Elephanta Island?
questions.explanation: The island also contains a small group of Buddhist rock-cut caves and a stupa mound in addition to the Hindu caves.

questions.q: From which Mumbai landmark do tourist ferries to Elephanta most commonly depart?
questions.explanation: Ferries to Elephanta Island typically set off from the jetty beside the Gateway of India.

questions.q: The dramatic panel showing Shiva killing the demon Andhaka is an example of which aspect of the deity portrayed at Elephanta?
options: His role as a household god
questions.explanation: The Andhakasura-vadha panel shows Shiva in his terrifying, destructive form slaying the demon Andhaka.

questions.q: The layout of the great Elephanta cave is notable for being organized around which feature?
options: A central courtyard open to the sky
Multiple pillared halls with a sanctum (linga shrine)
A spiral ramp ascending a tower
questions.explanation: The main cave has a pillared hall with a square shrine housing a Shiva linga, around which the sculptural panels are arranged.

questions.q: What is the standard name of the central object of worship enshrined in the sanctum of the main Elephanta cave?
options: A statue of Nandi the bull
questions.explanation: The square sanctum at the heart of the cave enshrines a linga, the aniconic symbol of Shiva.

questions.q: The three-headed Trimurti of Elephanta is also widely known by which Sanskrit name describing Shiva as the great lord?
questions.explanation: The Trimurti is commonly identified as Sadashiva or Maheshamurti, depicting Shiva as the supreme Great Lord.

questions.q: Many art historians attribute the patronage of the great Elephanta cave to rulers of which early western Indian dynasty?
questions.explanation: A widely held scholarly view links the main cave's patronage to the Kalachuri dynasty of the 6th century.

questions.q: Several Elephanta sculptures suffered damage during the colonial period, often attributed in tradition to soldiers of which European power who controlled the island?
questions.explanation: Tradition holds that Portuguese soldiers, who controlled the island, damaged several of the cave's sculptures.

questions.q: The panel depicting Shiva and Parvati's wedding at Elephanta is known by which name?
questions.explanation: The Kalyanasundara panel portrays the marriage (kalyana) of Shiva and Parvati.

questions.q: One Elephanta panel shows the multi-armed demon king straining to lift the mountain where Shiva and Parvati sit. Which demon is this?
questions.explanation: The Ravananugraha panel depicts Ravana attempting to shake or lift Mount Kailasa beneath Shiva and Parvati.

questions.q: Which Indian government body is chiefly responsible for the protection and maintenance of the Elephanta Caves today?
questions.explanation: The Archaeological Survey of India (ASI) manages and conserves the Elephanta Caves as a protected monument.

questions.q: A cultural festival of classical music and dance is held annually against the backdrop of the Elephanta Caves. What is it commonly called?
questions.explanation: The Elephanta Festival is an annual celebration of classical music and dance organized at the caves.

questions.q: The large stone elephant statue that gave the island its European name was later moved to a location in Mumbai. Where is it now displayed?
options: Jijamata Udyaan (Victoria Gardens / Mumbai Zoo grounds)
Chhatrapati Shivaji Maharaj Vastu Sangrahalaya entrance
questions.explanation: The original stone elephant was relocated to Mumbai and reassembled in the Jijamata Udyaan (the former Victoria Gardens) grounds.
